steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. Harris tells an ane\cdote about a rough Channel crossing — but notice that who else stayed well keeps changing: first the captain, then the second mate, then 'one other man,' then possibly no one. The one constant is that Harris himself is always the healthy one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Harris is worried that the captain will start to feel sick during this boat trip.: The passage says nothing about worry or a current trip. ✗
  • Choice B — Harris has a hard time remembering his first trip across the English Channel when his friends ask about it.: No friends ask him anything in the passage; the shifting details look deliberate, not like a memory lapse. ✗
  • Choice C — When Harris speaks of an earlier trip, he often changes the details but always brags about his own wellness.: Exactly what the passage shows — the companion changes every time, yet Harris is always well. ✓
  • Choice D — Harris spent most of his time with a particular passenger during a previous boat trip.: The passage never identifies a single passenger; the companion keeps changing. ✗
C is correct.
